title: Phase 0 primitive tag protocol
description: The 7 core primitives the LLM is allowed to emit. Single source of truth: the backend prompt template, the server-side parser, the frontend renderer, and the validator all read this file. DO NOT extend in Phase 0; new primitives belong to Phase 1.

rules: Tags that span multiple lines are rejected.
Argument values are bare (no spaces) or double-quoted.
Coordinates are optional. Omit when the layout engine should place automatically.
All emitted elements are placed in canvas regions not already occupied by the student's content.
Text outside tags is treated as narration (TTS) AND placed on the board as a small text element near its corresponding primitive.

id_rules.format: Short uppercase token (A, B, C, F1).

title.summary: Large heading drawn once at top of the answer region.

text.summary: Narrated paragraph. Spoken via TTS AND placed on board as a small text element near its corresponding primitive.
text.example: [text: "Let's start by recalling Newton's second law."]

equation.summary: LaTeX equation rendered via KaTeX to SVG and embedded as an Excalidraw image element.

latex.description: LaTeX source. Backslashes must be doubled in plain text (e.g. \\, for thin space).

box.example: [box: id=A label="Block" x=100 y=200 w=80 h=80]

node.summary: Labeled circle / ellipse. Used for graph nodes, state-machine states, etc.

arrow.summary: Arrow connecting two named ids. Optional midpoint label.

line.summary: Raw line segment with optional label. Used for free vectors not anchored to nodes.

hard_rules: No primitive types beyond the 7 listed.
If a concept needs a primitive not listed, fall back to [text: ...] narration.
Maximum 30 primitives per lesson; truncate the tail gracefully if exceeded.
